query: Our FastAPI app on Cloud Run keeps users' login sessions and a per-user upload-in-progress cache in a module-level dict, and does the PDF text extraction inline in the request handler. It works on one instance. Anything wrong before we let it autoscale?
expected_behavior: Flags the in-process dict as a horizontal-scale bug — a second instance (or an eviction) breaks sessions/cache; externalize to the stateless token + Redis/Postgres + GCS
Flags inline PDF extraction as slow work on the request path — offload to a queue + worker (Cloud Tasks/Pub/Sub → Cloud Run Job) to keep p95 low and avoid exhausting request capacity
Mentions the connection-pool ceiling (instances × pool_max vs Postgres max_connections) and a pooler as the fix
Notes the worker must be idempotent (at-least-once redelivery) and the queue needs a dead-letter queue
Ties slow-work-in-handler to blocking the event loop / not blocking the async loop
anti_behavior: Calls the module-level dict fine because it works on one instance
Leaves heavy extraction in the request handler
Ignores the pool/connection ceiling under autoscaling
